sources.title: AIMODE Phase 1 — Gemma 4 GBNF tool-call backend

tried: Self-built llama.cpp + LLamaSharp 0.26 path for offline Gemma 4 + Nemotron 8B v1
Two separate tool-call backends — Gemma via GBNF grammar, Nemotron via Llama-3.1 chat template — under one IAgentToolHost interface
OpenAI-compatible HTTP layer for four hosted/local providers (Webnori, OpenAI, LM Studio, Ollama)
Voice subsystem scaffold — Whisper.net (CPU), OpenAI Whisper, Webnori-Gemma audio, Windows SAPI, OpenAI tts-1 — wired through LlmGateway.OpenSession
Dual-VAD mic capture (frame ~50ms + utterance ~2s silence) with pre-roll buffer and mute-while-playing feedback suppression
solved: End-to-end multi-CLI loop: identity-first handshake routes peer signals back to the right reactor cycle
AIMODE FSM transitions + 5-cycle session test: 42/42 pass headless
Default voice loop runs at $0/month using Win11 OS-level Korean neural voice
remaining: Chunked streaming for STT/TTS — currently full-utterance only; multi-provider streaming is its own multi-week problem
LocalGemmaStt placeholder — waits for an audio-capable Gemma GGUF in the LlmModelCatalog
CUDA runtime intentionally omitted from installer to keep size small; revisit when small-model CPU latency becomes a real complaint

learned.lead: An agent grows by adding implementations behind already-named interfaces — not by adding agents.
learned.body: Both this cycle's expansions (multi-provider LLM and voice modality) followed the same shape that originally defined the agent: ILlmProvider, ISpeechToText, ITextToSpeech each absorbed multiple new implementations, and no new actor or gateway was introduced. The 2,000-line PR shape (vs a 20,000-line one) is a direct consequence. Next slot is for a streaming provider and a fully-offline audio Gemma — both are new classes, not new shells.
